mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase discount rates applied to equity cash flows, compressing present values and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in greater than 65 percent of months where FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack reduces corporate profit margins and consumer spending, triggering negative short-term equity returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 70 percent of observations where UNRATE crosses above 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts signal a synchronized contraction in consumer demand, leading to negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC greater than 0.05 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, a month-over-month decline in industrial production indicates supply-side constraints that compress corporate earnings, driving negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles yield positive 20-day returns in greater than 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation growth outpaces the federal funds rate, real rates turn negative, eroding purchasing power and triggering negative forward equity returns as markets price in delayed tightening.
failure_condition: Negative real rate environments produce positive 20-day returns in greater than 65 percent of months.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, generating positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to correlate with positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC greater than 0.02 across multiple economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly GDP growth falls below 1.0 percent, recession fears dominate market sentiment, causing immediate negative short-term equity returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 70 percent of quarters where GDPC1 growth drops below 1.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising employment and falling industrial production signals productivity deterioration and margin compression, resulting in negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS and INDPRO divergence yields positive 20-day returns in greater than 60 percent of observed months.
